subtopics.title: Comprehensive Revision of SS1-SS2 Topics
common_misconceptions: WAEC only tests SS2 topics (tests SS1-SS3 combined)
JAMB questions are completely different from WAEC
Skipping topics that seem difficult is a good strategy
subtopics.teaching_note: SS3 Mathematics consolidates all SS1-SS2 topics with focus on exam preparation. Key WAEC areas: Algebra (quadratic equations, simultaneous equations, logarithms), Geometry (circle theorems, trigonometry), Calculus (differentiation, integration, applications), Statistics (probability, mean, standard deviation). Worked examples should be practiced extensively. Common formulas to memorize: quadratic formula, sine/cosine rules, area of triangle, chain rule, integration by substitution.

subtopics.title: Mathematics Exam Strategies
common_misconceptions: Showing workings wastes time (it earns method marks)
Guessing is better than leaving blank
subtopics.teaching_note: Exam strategy: 1) Read all questions first. 2) Start with easiest questions. 3) Show all workings - WAEC awards marks for method even if final answer is wrong. 4) Check units (convert to consistent units). 5) Rounding: unless specified, give exact answers or 2-3 significant figures. 6) Always state formulas before substituting values. 7) Diagrams: draw clear diagrams for geometry questions. 8) Check: if time permits, verify answers by substitution.
